summary_en: Policy-held stock (at market value) as % of consolidated net assets to fall below 20% by end-FY3/28, accelerated 2 years; additional target to reach below 10% set for earlier achievement. Existing ¥20B (book value) reduction through end-FY3/27 maintained.
title_en: Revision of Policy-Held Stock Reduction Target
translation_en_brief: On 15 May 2026, Daishi Hokuetsu Financial Group's Board resolved to revise its policy-held stock reduction targets. The subsidiary Daishi Hokuetsu Bank will continue reducing policy-held stocks by ¥20B (book value) through end-FY3/27 (originally planned to end-FY3/27). The revised policy accelerates the timeline for policy-held equities (including deemed holdings, at market value) to represent less than 20% of consolidated net assets: this 20% target is now to be achieved by end-FY3/28, brought forward 2 years from the prior end-FY3/29 deadline. Additionally, the company has set a new longer-term target of reducing policy-held stocks to below 10% of consolidated net assets, to be achieved as early as practicable thereafter. The revision reflects the company's commitment to enhance capital efficiency and strengthen its financial position in line with the Corporate Governance Code.
